articles.headline: Kayla McBride and Olivia Miles lead Lynx to 6th straight win, 105-102 over Storm
articles.description: — Kayla McBride made six 3-pointers and scored 33 points, rookie Olivia Miles had 32 points, eight rebounds and eight assists, and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Seattle Storm 105-102 on Monday night for their sixth straight victory.

articles.headline: Kiki Iriafen scores 23 points as Mystics snap Valkyries' 9-game winning streak with 90-82 victory
articles.description: — Kiki Iriafen had 23 points and 12 rebounds, Shakira Austin finished with 15 points and 15 rebounds, and the Washington Mystics rallied past Golden State for a 90-82 victory Monday night to snap the Valkyries' nine-game winning streak.

articles.headline: A’ja Wilson and Jackie Young combine for 50 points to lead the Aces past the Tempo 109-83
articles.description: — A’ja Wilson scored 26 points, Jackie Young had a season-high 24 points to go with 12 assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat the Toronto Tempo 109-83 on Monday night.

images.caption: Jewell Loyd somehow gets the and-1 to fall

articles.headline: Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu lead Liberty to 99-98 OT win over short-handed Wings
articles.description: — Breanna Stewart had 33 points, 13 rebounds and eight assists, Sabrina Ionescu scored 12 of her 21 points in overtime, and the New York Liberty beat the short-handed Dallas Wings 99-98 on Monday night to snap a four-game skid.

article.headline: Fire hold the Mystics to 29% shooting in a 75-56 rout
article.description: — Carla Leite scored 14 points, Serah Williams had 12 points and the Portland Fire beat the Washington Mystics 75-56 on Thursday night.
article.linkText: Fire hold the Mystics to 29% shooting in a 75-56 rout

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Sonia Citron fights off defender for and-1) 2026/07/16 ESHEET

article.story: WASHINGTON -- — Carla Leite scored 14 points, Serah Williams had 12 points and the Portland Fire beat the Washington Mystics 75-56 on Thursday night. Sarah Ashlee Barker added 10 points to help Portland (11-14) avenge a quadruple-overtime loss to Washington on June 28. Frieda Buhner made all three of her 3-point attempts, and the Fire finished 10 of 35 from distance. Shakira Austin had 19 points and nine rebounds for Washington (12-11). The Mystics shot 29% from the field, going 2 of 21 from 3-point range, to go with 19 turnovers. Portland jumped out to a 28-13 lead after the first quarter and extended it to 43-20 by the midway point of the second. The Fire led by double figures the entire second half. Up next Fire: At Minnesota on Saturday night. Mystics: At Golden State on Saturday night. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
